_metadata.description: Curated EFSA OpenFoodTox reference data for harmful additive validation
_metadata.source: EFSA OpenFoodTox v9 (2024), EFSA Journal scientific opinions, JECFA evaluations
_metadata.update_instructions: Download latest from https://www.efsa.europa.eu/en/microstrategy/openfoodtox and run: python3 scripts/api_audit/verify_efsa.py --update-reference path/to/openfoodtox.csv

Acesulfame Potassium.notes: EFSA 2025 re-evaluation raised ADI from 9 to 15 mg/kg/day, aligning with FDA and JECFA

Aspartame.notes: IARC classified as 2B (possibly carcinogenic) in 2023; EFSA and JECFA maintained ADI of 40 mg/kg/day

Sucralose.notes: Stable at high temperatures. Some in-vitro genotoxicity concerns from sucralose-6-acetate metabolite but EFSA has not changed ADI

Saccharin.notes: Delisted from NTP Report on Carcinogens in 2000. Rat bladder tumour mechanism not applicable to humans

Sodium Nitrite.notes: Nitrosamine formation in vivo. IARC 2A classification for processed meat (nitrite + haem). EFSA confirmed ADI adequate but recommended reducing use levels

Sodium Nitrate.notes: Converts to nitrite in vivo. Same EFSA opinion as E250

Titanium Dioxide.notes: EFSA 2021 concluded genotoxicity could not be ruled out. EU banned E171 as food additive effective 2022. IARC 2B was for inhalation route only

BHA (Butylated Hydroxyanisole).notes: IARC 2B based on rodent forestomach tumours (not relevant to humans lacking forestomach). EFSA ADI 1.0 vs JECFA 0.5

BHT (Butylated Hydroxytoluene).notes: EFSA temporary ADI of 0.25. Hepatic effects at higher doses in rodents

Carrageenan.notes: ADI 'not specified' (acceptable at current use). Degraded carrageenan (poligeenan) is the concern, not food-grade E407

Tartrazine.notes: EU requires warning label on products containing tartrazine: 'may have an adverse effect on activity and attention in children'

Allura Red AC.notes: Southampton study linked to hyperactivity in children; EU warning label required

Brilliant Blue FCF.notes: EFSA ADI lower than JECFA due to stricter interpretation of renal effects in rodents

Sunset Yellow FCF.notes: Part of Southampton Six azo dyes linked to child hyperactivity

Erythrosine.notes: Restricted to cocktail/candied cherries in EU. Thyroid effects at high doses in rats

Sodium Benzoate.notes: Can form benzene when combined with ascorbic acid under heat/UV. EFSA concluded no safety concern at current use levels

Potassium Sorbate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2019 re-evaluation (revised group ADI from 3.0 to 11.0 mg/kg/day as sorbic acid)
Potassium Sorbate.notes: EFSA 2019 revised group ADI upward from 3.0 to 11.0 mg/kg/day as sorbic acid (previous 2015 opinion was more conservative). JECFA ADI remains 25 mg/kg/day

Propylparaben.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2004 — no longer authorised as food additive since 2006
Propylparaben.notes: EU removed E217 authorization in 2006 due to reproductive toxicity concerns (anti-androgenic effects)

Sulfur Dioxide / Sulfites.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2016;14(4):4438 (group ADI as SO2 equivalents)
Sulfur Dioxide / Sulfites.notes: Major allergen. EU mandatory declaration above 10 mg/kg. Sulfite sensitivity affects ~1% of population, higher in asthmatics

TBHQ (tert-Butylhydroquinone).notes: In vitro genotoxicity positive in some studies; in vivo negative. Immune modulation concerns from Cheryl Rockwell research (2021)

Phosphoric Acid.notes: EFSA lowered group ADI for phosphates from 70 to 40 mg/kg/day in 2019. Concerns about renal and cardiovascular effects

Monosodium Glutamate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2017;15(7):4910 (group ADI for glutamic acid and glutamates)
Monosodium Glutamate.notes: EFSA established group ADI of 30 mg/kg/day in 2017 (JECFA had set 'not specified'). Based on developmental toxicity

Acrylamide.notes: Process contaminant, not additive. EFSA confirmed MOE approach, no safe level. EU Regulation 2017/2158 sets benchmark levels

Polysorbate 80.notes: Emulsifier. Some in-vitro evidence of gut barrier disruption and microbiome effects at high doses

Silicon Dioxide.notes: EFSA 2018 stated E551 could not be assessed due to insufficient characterisation of nano-sized particles. Re-evaluation ongoing

Calcium Propionate.notes: ADI 'not specified' (no concern at current use levels). Some studies suggest irritability/restlessness in children at high intakes

Magnesium Stearate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2018;16(3):5236 (group assessment, fatty acid salts)
Magnesium Stearate.notes: Widely used flow agent. ADI 'not specified'. Bioavailability interference claims not substantiated at supplement doses

Xanthan Gum.notes: ADI 'not specified'. Thickener/stabilizer. Necrotising enterocolitis risk in premature infants led to FDA advisory (SimplyThick)

Cellulose / Microcrystalline Cellulose.notes: ADI 'not specified'. EFSA 2018 concluded no safety concern. Nano-cellulose particles data gap noted

Steviol Glycosides.notes: Expressed as steviol equivalents. Natural high-intensity sweetener from Stevia rebaudiana

Neotame.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2025:9480 (re-evaluation raised ADI from 2.0 to 10.0)
Neotame.notes: Aspartame derivative ~7000x sweeter than sucrose. EFSA 2025 re-evaluation raised ADI from 2.0 to 10.0 mg/kg/day. Not a significant phenylalanine source (unlike aspartame)

Advantame.notes: ~20,000x sweeter than sucrose. Not a significant phenylalanine source

Cyclamate.notes: Banned in the US since 1969 (bladder cancer in rats). EU and most other countries approve it. EFSA ADI more conservative than JECFA

Calcium Disodium EDTA.efsa_adi_source: JECFA 1977, maintained by SCF/EFSA. Re-evaluation call for data issued 2024.
Calcium Disodium EDTA.notes: Chelating agent. ADI 2.5 mg/kg bw/day from JECFA 1977. EFSA re-evaluation pending (call for data 2024). May affect mineral bioavailability

Polydextrose.notes: Soluble fiber/bulking agent. ADI 'not specified'. Laxative effects above ~90g/day

Carboxymethylcellulose (CMC).notes: ADI 'not specified'. Emulsifier. Georgia State University research linked CMC + P80 to gut inflammation in mouse models

Polyethylene Glycol (PEG).notes: Polymer. EU restricted to tablet coating and capsule use. PEG antibody allergic reactions reported with PEGylated drugs/vaccines

Azodicarbonamide.efsa_adi_source: Not evaluated by EFSA (not authorised in EU)
Azodicarbonamide.notes: Banned in EU, Australia, some Asian countries. FDA permits up to 45 ppm in US. Decomposes to semicarbazide (potential carcinogen)

Potassium Bromate.notes: Banned in EU, UK, Canada, China, Brazil. FDA still permits in US. IARC Group 2B. Renal cell tumours in rats

Brominated Vegetable Oil (BVO).notes: Banned in EU and Japan. FDA revoked GRAS status July 2024. Bromine bioaccumulation, thyroid effects, and cardiac effects in animal studies

Red 3 (Erythrosine).notes: FDA revoked Red 3 authorization Jan 2025 (thyroid tumours in male rats). EU restricted to cocktail/candied cherries only

Propyl Gallate.notes: Antioxidant preservative. EFSA ADI lower than JECFA. Some estrogenic activity in vitro

Hexane.efsa_adi_source: Not a food additive (extraction solvent residue)
Hexane.notes: EU Directive 2009/32/EC sets max residue limits for extraction solvents. Neurotoxicity from chronic occupational exposure. Traces in processed oils

Guar Gum.notes: ADI 'not specified'. Soluble fiber. Esophageal obstruction risk with guar gum diet products (banned for that use in US/EU)

Locust Bean Gum.notes: ADI 'not specified'. Thickener from carob seeds. Well-tolerated

Maltodextrin.efsa_adi_source: Not classified as food additive in EU (classified as food ingredient)
Maltodextrin.notes: High GI ingredient (DE 3-20). Not an additive per EU definition. Gut microbiome disruption at high intakes in animal models

Annatto (Bixin / Norbixin).notes: Natural colorant from annatto seeds. EFSA set separate ADIs for bixin (6.0) and norbixin (0.3) due to different toxicology profiles

Caramel Color (Class III and IV).notes: 4-MEI contaminant in Class III/IV is the concern (IARC 2B). California Prop 65 listed 4-MEI. EFSA ADI applies to the whole caramel color

Soy Lecithin.notes: ADI 'not specified'. Major allergen (soy). Hexane extraction residues are the primary chemical concern

High Fructose Corn Syrup.efsa_adi_source: Not a food additive (classified as sugar/sweetener ingredient)
High Fructose Corn Syrup.notes: Not regulated as additive. EU isoglucose production quota removed 2017. Metabolic concerns well-documented (NAFLD, insulin resistance)

Hydrogenated Starch Hydrolysate.notes: Sugar alcohol mixture. ADI 'not specified'. Laxative effects at high doses

Partially Hydrogenated Oils.notes: EU Regulation 2019/649 limits industrial trans fats to 2g per 100g fat. FDA eliminated GRAS status 2018. WHO REPLACE target: global elimination by 2023

Lead (as contaminant).notes: No safe threshold. EFSA withdrew PTWI in 2010, replaced with BMDL approach. Commission Regulation (EU) 2023/915 sets maximum levels

Cadmium (as contaminant).notes: IARC Group 1 human carcinogen (lung). EFSA TWI 2.5 ug/kg bw/week. Renal tubular damage. Cocoa/chocolate significant dietary source

Mercury / Methylmercury (as contaminant).notes: Neurodevelopmental toxicant. Fish/seafood main dietary source. EU Regulation 2023/915 sets maximum levels in fish

Arsenic (as contaminant).efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2009;7(10):1351 (BMDL01 range 0.3-8 ug/kg bw/day for inorganic As)
Arsenic (as contaminant).notes: IARC Group 1 carcinogen (skin, lung, bladder). Inorganic arsenic is the concern. Rice significant dietary source. EU max levels for rice

Indigo Carmine.notes: FD&C Blue No. 2 / E132. EFSA 2014 re-evaluation confirmed ADI of 5 mg/kg/day. 2023 follow-up maintained the ADI

Erythritol.notes: ADI 'not specified' (no safety concern at current use levels). Sugar alcohol / polyol. Cleveland Clinic 2023 study raised cardiovascular thrombosis concerns but EFSA has not changed assessment

Sorbic Acid.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2019;17(8):5625 (follow-up, revised group ADI for sorbic acid + potassium sorbate)
Sorbic Acid.notes: Group ADI with E202 (potassium sorbate) and E203 (calcium sorbate). EFSA 2019 revised EU ADI to 11.0 from temporary 3.0 (2015). JECFA maintains original 25 mg/kg/day (1973, never revised by JECFA)

Sorbitol.efsa_adi_source: EFSA re-evaluation ongoing (call for genotoxicity data 2023)
Sorbitol.notes: ADI 'not specified' historically. EFSA issued call for genotoxicity data in 2023 as part of sweeteners re-evaluation. Laxative effects above ~50g/day

Stearic Acid.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2017;15(3):4785 (re-evaluation of fatty acids E570)
Stearic Acid.notes: ADI 'not specified' (group assessment for fatty acids). Common supplement excipient. No safety concern at current use levels

Iron Oxide.notes: ADI 'not specified' historically. EFSA 2015 could not establish ADI due to insufficient toxicity data but no safety concern at current use levels. Nano-particle characterisation data gap noted

Propylene Glycol.notes: Solvent/humectant. ADI 25 mg/kg/day. Used in pharmaceutical and food formulations

Maltitol.notes: Sugar alcohol. ADI 'not specified'. Laxative effects above ~30-50g/day. Glycemic impact lower than sucrose but not zero

Maltol.notes: Flavour enhancer. Naturally occurring in malt, bread crust, roasted coffee. Low concern at typical use levels

Sodium Metabisulfite.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2016;14(4):4438 (group ADI as SO2 equivalents, same as E220-E228)
Sodium Metabisulfite.notes: Part of sulfite group (E220-E228). Same group ADI of 0.7 mg/kg/day as SO2 equivalents. Major allergen

Sodium Sulfite.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2016;14(4):4438 (group ADI as SO2 equivalents, same as E220-E228)
Sodium Sulfite.notes: Part of sulfite group (E220-E228). Same group ADI of 0.7 mg/kg/day as SO2 equivalents

Potassium Nitrite.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2017;15(6):4786 (same opinion as E250 sodium nitrite)
Potassium Nitrite.notes: Same group as E250 (sodium nitrite). ADI 0.07 mg/kg/day as nitrite ion. Nitrosamine formation concern

Potassium Nitrate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2017;15(6):4786 (same opinion as E251 sodium nitrate)
Potassium Nitrate.notes: Same group as E251 (sodium nitrate). ADI 3.7 mg/kg/day as nitrate ion. Converts to nitrite in vivo

Potassium Benzoate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2016;14(3):4433 (group ADI with E210/E211 as benzoic acid)
Potassium Benzoate.notes: Same group ADI as E210 (benzoic acid) and E211 (sodium benzoate). Benzene formation risk with ascorbic acid

Sorbitan Monostearate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2017;15(5):4788 (group ADI for sorbitan esters E491-E495)
Sorbitan Monostearate.notes: Emulsifier. Group ADI for sorbitan esters. No safety concern at current use levels

Shellac.notes: Natural resin coating/glazing agent. ADI 'not specified'. Used in supplement tablet/capsule coatings

Methylparaben.efsa_adi_source: SCF/EFSA group ADI for methyl and ethyl parabens
Methylparaben.notes: E218 is APPROVED in EU food (unlike E217 propylparaben which was removed in 2006). Group ADI 10 mg/kg/day for methyl/ethyl parabens

Thaumatin.notes: Natural protein sweetener from katemfe fruit. ~2000x sweeter than sucrose. ADI 'not specified' — no safety concern

Fast Green FCF.efsa_adi_source: Not authorised as food additive in EU (no E143 evaluation)
Fast Green FCF.notes: FD&C Green No. 3. Not permitted in EU. FDA approved in US. JECFA ADI 25 mg/kg/day

Cochineal / Carmine.notes: Natural red colorant from cochineal insects. Major allergen (insect protein). EFSA 2015 re-evaluation confirmed ADI

Monoglycerides and Diglycerides.notes: ADI 'not specified'. Emulsifier class. Includes soy monoglycerides. No safety concern at current use levels

Diphosphates / Triphosphates / Polyphosp.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2019;17(6):5674 (group ADI for phosphates E338-E452, expressed as phosphorus)
Diphosphates / Triphosphates / Polyphosp.notes: Group ADI 40 mg P/kg/day (EFSA 2019, lowered from JECFA 70). Covers E450 (diphosphates), E451 (triphosphates), E452 (polyphosphates). Exposure exceeds ADI in children

Calcium Silicate.notes: ADI 'not specified'. Anti-caking agent. EFSA 2018 concluded no safety concern at current use but noted insufficient nano-particle characterisation data

Polysorbate 40.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2015;13(7):4152 (group ADI for polysorbates E432-E436)
Polysorbate 40.notes: Part of polysorbate group (E432-E436). Same group ADI as polysorbate 80 (E433)

Polysorbate 65.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2015;13(7):4152 (group ADI for polysorbates E432-E436)
Polysorbate 65.notes: Part of polysorbate group (E432-E436). Same group ADI as polysorbate 80 (E433)

Sodium Aluminium Phosphate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2018;16(7):5372 (re-evaluation with aluminium sulphates E520-E523)
Sodium Aluminium Phosphate.notes: Aluminium-containing additive. Contributes to total aluminium intake. EFSA TWI for aluminium is 1 mg Al/kg bw/week

Calcium Aluminium Phosphate.efsa_adi_source: No dedicated EFSA evaluation. Covered under aluminium phosphate group.
Calcium Aluminium Phosphate.notes: Aluminium-containing additive. Less commonly used than E541. Subject to overall aluminium TWI

Fatty Acid Polyglycerol Esters.notes: Emulsifier. ADI 25 mg/kg/day. No safety concern at current use levels

Sodium Hexametaphosphate.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2019;17(6):5674 (group ADI for phosphates, expressed as phosphorus)
Sodium Hexametaphosphate.notes: Part of phosphate group (E452). Same group ADI as E338/E450/E451

Antimony.efsa_adi_source: EFSA used WHO TDI of 0.006 mg/kg/day for antimony trioxide
Antimony.notes: Regulated under EU 2023/915 for contaminant maximum levels. Migration from packaging materials is the primary dietary source

Bisphenol S.efsa_adi_source: No dedicated EFSA ADI. Commission Regulation (EU) 2024/3190 bans BPS in food contact materials.
Bisphenol S.notes: Banned in EU food contact materials alongside BPA and BPF (Regulation 2024/3190). Endocrine disruptor. No ADI — approach is exposure minimisation

Bisphenol F.efsa_adi_source: No dedicated EFSA ADI. Commission Regulation (EU) 2024/3190 bans BPF in food contact materials.
Bisphenol F.notes: Banned in EU food contact materials alongside BPA and BPS (Regulation 2024/3190). Endocrine disruptor

Senna.efsa_adi_source: EFSA Journal 2018 + EFSA 2024: hydroxyanthracene derivatives (HADs) are genotoxic and carcinogenic concern
Senna.notes: EFSA 2018 and 2024: HADs (sennosides, aloe-emodin, emodin) are genotoxic. EU attempted to restrict; some member states have banned in food supplements. Used medicinally as stimulant laxative

Polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP).notes: Polymer. ADI 'not specified'. Used as clarifying agent in wine/beverages and as pharma excipient

Crospovidone.notes: Cross-linked PVP. Pharma disintegrant and food additive. ADI 'not specified'. No safety concern at current use levels
